vek.description: The Vek are giant insect-like monsters that are the primary antagonists. They come in regular and Alpha variants.
vek.alpha_rules: Alpha Vek have higher HP, higher damage, and sometimes additional attack effects. They replace regular Vek as the game progresses or on higher difficulties.

categories.name: Firefly

regular.attack: Launches a volatile mass of goo at a single tile in a straight line

alpha.attack: Launches a larger mass of goo dealing heavy damage

categories.notes: Basic ranged Vek. Common early-game enemy.

categories.name: Hornet

regular.attack: Stabs single adjacent tile

alpha.attack: Stabs 2 tiles in front of the unit

categories.notes: Fast flying melee unit. Cannot be drowned in water or pushed into chasms due to flying.

categories.name: Scarab

regular.attack: Lobs an artillery shot at a single tile for 1 damage (5 tile range)

alpha.attack: Lobs a powerful artillery shot at a single tile for 3 damage (5 tile range)

categories.notes: Artillery Vek. Can shoot over obstacles. Weak but annoying.

categories.name: Leaper

regular.attack: Leaps to destination avoiding obstacles. Webs an adjacent target, then stabs it for 3 damage during enemy turn.

alpha.attack: Leaps to destination. Webs an adjacent target, then stabs it for 5 damage during enemy turn.

categories.notes: Extremely dangerous despite low HP. Webs prevent mech movement. Kill on sight. Cannot spawn alongside Scorpions.

categories.name: Scorpion

regular.attack: Webs an adjacent target, preparing to stab it for 1 damage.

alpha.attack: Webs an adjacent target, preparing to stab it for 3 damage.

categories.notes: Dangerous melee Vek that restricts movement with webs. Cannot spawn alongside Leapers.

categories.name: Centipede

regular.attack: Shoots goo that damages target and applies A.C.I.D.

alpha.attack: Shoots goo dealing more damage, applying A.C.I.D. to nearby tiles

categories.notes: Applies A.C.I.D. which doubles subsequent weapon damage. Very dangerous combined with other Vek.

categories.name: Spider

regular.attack: Does not attack directly. Throws a Spiderling Egg onto any tile in its row/column. Egg webs adjacent units and hatches into a Spiderling next turn.

alpha.attack: Throws eggs that spawn stronger Spiderlings

spiderling.notes: Weak but adds to enemy count. Good for chain/arachnoid combos.

categories.notes: Rare support Vek. Priority target — eggs multiply the enemy count rapidly.

categories.name: Beetle

regular.attack: Charges forward in a straight line, damaging and pushing the first target

alpha.attack: Heavier charge dealing major damage with push

categories.notes: Tanky charger. Push to redirect charge direction. Dies in water despite high HP.

categories.name: Blobber

regular.attack: Launches a blob to target tile. Blob damages all adjacent tiles next turn.

alpha.attack: Throws an Alpha Blob with larger explosion damage

blob.description: Lands on target tile. Next turn, explodes dealing damage to all 4 adjacent tiles. Can be pushed or killed before detonation.

categories.notes: Delayed damage dealer. Blobs can be pushed away or destroyed before they explode.

categories.name: Digger

regular.attack: Creates a defensive rock wall, then attacks adjacent tiles

alpha.attack: Creates rock wall and attacks with more damage

categories.notes: Creates terrain obstacles. Rock walls can block your movement but also block Vek charges.

categories.name: Burrower

regular.attack: Slams against 3 tiles in a row, hitting each. Retreats underground after taking damage.

alpha.attack: Heavier slam on 3 tiles

categories.notes: Cannot be pushed or moved. Retreats underground when damaged (extinguishes fire). Very annoying.

categories.name: Moth

regular.attack: Launches an artillery attack for 1 damage, pushing shooter and target.

alpha.attack: Launches an artillery attack for 3 damage, pushing shooter and target.

categories.notes: Advanced Edition flying artillery. Self-push can cause collateral damage to units behind it. Cannot be drowned.

categories.name: Bouncer

regular.attack: Deals 1 damage to the target then bounces backwards.

alpha.attack: Deals 3 damage to the target then bounces backwards.

categories.notes: Advanced Edition Vek. Flipping doesn't redirect damage. Self-push causes additional collateral.

categories.name: Bot

regular.attack: High-tech weapons causing fire and large-area damage

alpha.attack: Upgraded weapons

categories.notes: Not actually Vek — sentient AI robots from Pinnacle Island. 1 HP but dangerous attacks.

categories.name: Crab

regular.attack: Launches artillery attack on 2 tiles for 1 damage (5 tile range).

alpha.attack: Launches artillery attack on 2 tiles for 2 damage (5 tile range).

categories.notes: Base game artillery Vek. Hits 2 tiles simultaneously.

categories.name: Volatile Vek

regular.attack: Explodes on death, dealing damage to adjacent tiles. Does not move or attack on its own.

categories.notes: Stationary explosive Vek. Can be pushed into enemies or buildings. Explodes when killed or bumped.

categories.name: Gastropod

regular.attack: Grapple for 1 damage, pulling the Vek towards the target.

alpha.attack: Grapple for 2 damage, pulling the Vek towards the target.

categories.notes: Advanced Edition. Pulls itself towards target after attacking. Unique pull-self mechanic.

categories.name: Mosquito

regular.attack: Smokes the target, preparing to whip it for 1 damage.

alpha.attack: Smokes the target, preparing to whip it for 2 damage.

categories.notes: Advanced Edition flying melee. Applies smoke to target before attacking, cancelling mech attacks.

categories.name: Plasmodia

regular.attack: Launches a short-lived Spore that explodes next turn, dealing damage to adjacent tiles.

alpha.attack: Launches a more powerful Spore.

categories.notes: Advanced Edition. Spawns Spore minions that explode next turn. Similar to Blobber but with different mechanics.

categories.name: Starfish

regular.attack: Deals 1 damage to four diagonal tiles.

alpha.attack: Deals 2 damage to four diagonal tiles.

categories.notes: Advanced Edition. Unique diagonal attack pattern — hits the 4 oblique tiles instead of cardinal.

categories.name: Tumblebug

regular.attack: Creates an explosive boulder, preparing to destroy it next turn by attacking it for 1 damage.

alpha.attack: Creates an explosive boulder and attacks it for 2 damage.

categories.notes: Advanced Edition. Creates and detonates boulders. Boulder explosions can damage buildings.

psions.description: Psions are special support Vek that provide global buffs to all other Vek on the map. They do not attack directly but move around the battlefield. Killing a Psion removes its buff.

types.name: Soldier Psion
types.effect: +1 HP to all other Vek while alive
types.notes: Makes all Vek harder to kill. Kill early if possible.

types.name: Blood Psion
types.effect: All Vek regenerate 1 HP per turn
types.notes: Counters chip damage strategies. Must kill quickly or enemies heal faster than you damage.

types.name: Shell Psion
types.effect: All Vek gain Armored (take 1 less damage from attacks)
types.notes: Drastically reduces damage output. Priority target. A.C.I.D. counters armor.

types.name: Blast Psion
types.effect: All Vek explode on death, dealing 1 damage to adjacent tiles
types.notes: Can chain-kill weakened Vek near each other. Dangerous near buildings.

types.name: Smoldering Psion
types.effect: All other Vek are protected from fire and leave fire on tiles when killed.
types.notes: Advanced Edition. Counters fire-based squads entirely. Kill to re-enable fire strategies.

types.name: Arachnid Psion
types.effect: All Vek spawn a Spiderling on death
types.notes: Advanced Edition. Every kill creates a Spiderling. Can overwhelm with numbers.

types.name: Raging Psion
types.effect: All Vek gain Boosted (+1 damage on next attack)
types.notes: Advanced Edition. Significantly increases threat level. +1 damage across all Vek is devastating.

types.name: Psion Tyrant
types.effect: All player mechs take 1 damage at the end of every enemy turn
types.notes: Appears in the final battle. Constant drain on mech HP.

bosses.description: Boss/Leader Vek appear at the end of each island as the final battle's primary threat. They have higher HP, stronger attacks, and special properties.

types.name: Beetle Leader
types.attack: Charges dealing heavy damage, sets every tile in its path on fire
types.notes: Very tanky. Fire trail creates persistent hazard.

types.name: Firefly Leader
types.attack: Launches goo projectiles in TWO directions simultaneously
types.notes: Double-shot makes it hard to block both attacks.

types.name: Hornet Leader
types.attack: Stabs three tiles in a row, can fly
types.notes: Flying boss. Wide attack pattern. Cannot be drowned.

types.name: Scorpion Leader
types.attack: Webs all adjacent targets, then damages all adjacent tiles
types.notes: Highest HP boss. Webs restrict your mechs. Keep distance.

types.name: Spider Leader
types.attack: Spawns multiple Spiderlings. Webs extensive area.
types.notes: Spawns many Spiderlings. Good for achievements requiring kills (chain attack, etc).

types.name: Psion Abomination
types.attack: Does not attack directly. All other Vek gain +1 HP, Regeneration, and explode on death.
types.notes: Triple Psion effect boss. All Vek gain +1 HP, regenerate, AND explode. Nightmarish if not killed fast.

types.name: Large Goo
types.attack: Attempts to squish an adjacent tile, dealing 4 damage. Splits into Medium Goo on death.
types.notes: Splits into 2 Medium Goo (2 HP) on death, which split into Small Goo (1 HP). Total of 7 units if fully split.

spawning.description: New Vek emerge from underground through spawn points visible as ground cracks.
rules: Emerging Vek are telegraphed by visible ground cracks on tiles
Vek emerge at the END of the enemy turn, after attacks resolve
Blocking a spawn point (standing on it with a mech, unit, or boulder) prevents emergence and deals 1 damage to the blocked Vek
A blocked spawn does NOT kill the Vek — the spawn point persists until the Vek emerges or the battle ends
Frozen units on spawn points also block emergence
Emerging Vek on acid tiles gain A.C.I.D. status
Emerging Vek on fire tiles catch fire (exception: Burrowers lose fire underground)
Number of spawns increases in later turns and on higher difficulties
